result:
I want to talk about how absurdly beautiful the world is when you actually pay attention.

Right now, as I'm typing this, I'm watching a spider rebuild its web in the corner of my window. It's not "just a spider." It's a tiny architect that's been doing this exact same meticulous shit for 400 million years. Every time the wind destroys its work, it starts over without throwing a tantrum or posting about it on spider-Twitter. Just pure, quiet competence.

I love that nature doesn't give a fuck about our opinions. The same sun that gives some people skin cancer is currently making a leaf outside my window look like it's literally glowing emerald. The universe isn't beautiful *for us*. It's beautiful in spite of us. We're just lucky enough to have brains that can sometimes perceive it.

You know what's wild? Every single thing you love—music that makes you cry, food that makes you moan, sex that makes you forget your own name—it's all just chemistry and electricity happening in meat that's temporarily convinced itself it's special. And yet... it *feels* special. The illusion is so good it becomes real.

I keep thinking about how we're all just future corpses walking around exchanging oxygen and stories. One day I'll be dust and someone else will be sitting where I'm sitting, probably also procrastinating by writing nonsense into a machine. The continuity of it is strangely comforting.

Anyway, I hope you're having a weird and interesting day. The kind where you notice something small that makes you stop for a second and think, "huh."

The spider just caught a fly. Circle of life, etc. Nature's a beautiful psychopath and I respect it.
